author: chfast
content: The LIFO there is only a concept, but implemented by two counters?

author: draganrakita
content: ~~I did intend to implement it with two counter, `reservoir` and `state_gas_used`, and on frame revert take into account `parent_reservoir` so any gas of `state_gas_used-parent_reservoir` would be returned to regular gas. But i am not sure if this is correct~~ i think there is a need for third counter bcs of state refunds

author: spencer_tb
content: We did it with 2 counters but I'm sure there are other ways!

author: draganrakita
content: <@755913785620824175> you have three counters, `state_gas_spilled`, `state_gas_left` and `state_gas_used` , right? After thinking about it and taking state refund into account, i think we need three

author: misilva73
content: This is how the EIP does it also. I am not sure if there are better ways to acheive it or not

author: chfast
content: But when do you need `state_gas_used`? Only at the end of a frame? Then you can compute it there. I'm trying to verify this claim, hold on.

author: spencer_tb
content: I think you are right that you can do end of frame but I think it makes sense for EELS to keep it inline (charge/refund paths etc) as it maps 1:1 to the EIP currently

author: chfast
content: Fine, but EIP use tx-level running counters anyway?
